articles.title: Rice University Chemist Gets 15-Year Federal Ban for Data Fabrication — ORI Finally Acts After 17 Years
articles.summary: The Office of Research Integrity handed down a 15-year debarment to former Rice University chemist Ariel Fernández for intentionally fabricating data. The misconduct allegations surfaced 17 years ago, revealing how glacially slow the federal watchdog moves against scientific fraud.

articles.title: Lab Fraud Reform Derailed by Three Fatal Myths — Healthcare Blog Exposes CMS Loopholes

articles.summary: Despite acetaminophen being safer than ibuprofen for most patients, the FDA's liability fears and per-drug labeling mandate prevent comparative safety information from reaching consumers. This regulatory cowardice leaves patients in the dark about which painkiller actually poses less risk to their stomach, heart, and kidneys.
